note:
根据 The Intercept 审查的内部数据，茶党爱国者是一个主要的保守组织，自称是右翼最大的草根团体之一，实际上近年来得到了三位超级富豪的大力支持。最大的捐助者是德克萨斯州的亿万富翁克里斯托弗·戈德斯伯里，他于 1994 年将莎莎酱公司 Pace Foods 出售给金宝汤而发了财。 2019 年 9 月 11 日，戈德斯伯里通过电汇向 TPP 基金会捐赠了 100 万美元。根据税务文件，TPP 基金会当年的收入为 120 万美元。 Goldsbury 自 2014 年以来一直是 TPP 成员，并且在前几年已经向 TPP 的三个独立组织捐赠了 20,000 美元。 Goldsbury 没有回应置评请求。与此同时，该组织成员的活动似乎有所减弱。 The Intercept 发现在线数据中只有 144,000 名成员被标记为“活跃”，而 TPP 网站上声称的“网络有 300 万活跃分子”、“超过 300 万支持者”和“超过 300 万爱国者”。来自当地分会的数据显示，成员聚集在快速发展的地区，如科罗拉多州和整个太阳带，从加利福尼亚州到亚利桑那州、德克萨斯州、乔治亚州和佛罗里达州。 327 GB 的 TPP 数据是由一名声称入侵了该组织网络后端的消息人士匿名提供给 The Intercept 的。 1 月，The Intercept 获得的文件暴露了少数富有的 TPP 捐助者的身份，但新数据充实了对该组织巨额资金支持的理解。这些数据包括关于茶党爱国者当地分会成员、签署请愿书或捐赠的人的大量信息：他们的姓名、电话号码、家庭住址以及每个用户的详细活动历史记录。除了该组织的三个最大捐助者（其中至少有两个据报道是亿万富翁）之外，Intercept 不会点名或以其他方式披露该组织的个人成员的信息。由于黑客获取的数据仅来自该集团的网络基础设施，因此可能会丢失重要记录。例如，可能有 TPP 支持者在现场活动中注册或亲自捐款但未被网络数据库跟踪。黑客提供的一些数据得到了公开信息的证实，包括一些捐赠和 TPP 请愿书。尽管如此，验证所有数据还是不可能的，在 The Intercept 获得数据后，黑客更改了 TPP 网站上的页面。 TPP 没有回答有关违规行为的具体问题，而是向 The Intercept 提供了一封电子邮件，该电子邮件由联合创始人 Jenny Beth Martin 发送给成员，通知他们遭到黑客攻击，并补充说该组织已联系执法部门并努力“确保我们的系统是不会受到损害，并得到进一步保护，以确保此类事件不会再次发生。”电子邮件继续说道，“而且你可以肯定，我们将采取一切可能的措施来寻找并帮助起诉这些闯入我们电子家庭并窃取专有和机密信息的罪犯。”
TPP 成立于 2009 年，就在奥巴马总统就职后不久。据众多报道称，该组织的灵感来自 CNBC 编辑里克·桑特利 (Rick Santelli) 反对奥巴马政府在金融危机初期帮助房主避免丧失抵押品赎回权的提议。 TPP 在最初几年组织起来反对《平价医疗法案》和一般政府支出；今天，控制联邦支出仍然是该集团既定优先事项的核心。但该组织内部经常出现种族和反移民的敌意，该组织还参与组织了“三月拯救美国”集会，最终于 2021 年 1 月 6 日袭击美国国会大厦，旨在阻止国会认证乔。拜登的选举胜利。 （TPP 表示它没有为集会提供资金，并表示“对 1 月 6 日事件的转变感到震惊、愤怒和悲伤”，谴责暴力行为。）来自被黑数据库的记录揭示了其主要支持者的身份。现已去世的加州房地产大亨桑福德迪勒是另一位为 TPP 提供大量资金的亿万富翁。根据税务文件，TPP 基金会在 2015 年的收入为 106,318 美元。而根据被黑数据，他们当年只接受了两笔捐款，其中一笔是来自迪勒的 10 万美元支票。迪勒在 2016 年又向基金会捐赠了 10 万美元，2017 年又捐赠了 5 万美元。 2016 年，他还向 TPP 的超级 PAC 捐赠了 15 万美元。 Intercept 今年早些时候报道了迪勒的一些基金会捐款，去年年底美国广播公司新闻表示，司法部的文件涉及迪勒参与一项秘密游说计划，将政治捐款交易给与前总统唐纳德特朗普有关联的实体以获得赦免。 TPP 的另一位主要资助者是俄勒冈州的自由主义者大卫·戈尔，他的家族拥有 Gore-Tex 面料公司。根据 The Intercept 获得的内部数据，在 2018 年至 2021 年 1 月期间，他向 TPP Action 捐赠了 50,000 美元，向 TPP 的超级 PAC 捐赠了 275,000 美元，向 TPP 基金会捐赠了 124,000 美元。无法联系到戈尔置评。 Tea Party Patriots 拥有三个独立的组织：一个名为 TPP 基金会的 501(c)(3) 公共慈善机构；一个 501(c)(4) 社会福利组织，被允许从事比 501(c)(3) 更广泛的游说活动，称为 TPP 行动；还有一个超级政治行动委员会，它可以花无限量的黑钱来支持政治候选人，叫做TPP公民基金。被黑的数据包括个人向这三个组织捐款的信息，但不包括从利益集团和公司筹集的资金。例如，TPP 的超级 PAC 总共筹集了 290 万美元以支持特朗普的 2020 年竞选活动，但来自被黑数据的个人捐助者记录在那个选举周期中仅加起来 460,000 美元。被黑的记录还表明，尽管 TPP 树立了群众运动的形象，但从 2013 年或更早开始，只有不到 100 万人加入了当地分会，甚至刚刚签署了在线请愿书。在这些成员中，大约有三分之一被标记为“活跃”。数据描述了大约 800 个本地分会，包括每个分会的成员列表。本地 TPP 分会共有 15,000 名被标记为活跃用户，这意味着 TPP 数据库中只有大约 10% 的活跃用户是本地分会的成员——其他所有人都是签署请愿书、捐赠或订阅邮件列表的人。
这张基于被黑数据的地图显示了至少有 10 个用户的城市有多少活跃的茶党爱国者用户居住在哪些城市。
note_en:
Tea Party Patriots, a major conservative organization that bills itself as one of the largest grassroots groups on the right, was in fact heavily backed by three ultra-wealthy individuals in recent years, according to internal data reviewed by The Intercept.
 The largest donor was Texas billionaire Christopher Goldsbury, who made his fortune selling the salsa company Pace Foods to Campbell Soup in 1994. On September 11, 2019, Goldsbury donated $1 million to the TPP Foundation via wire transfer. According to tax documents, the TPP Foundation took in $1.2 million in revenue that year. Goldsbury had been a TPP member since 2014 and had already donated $20,000 to TPP’s three separate organizations in previous years. Goldsbury did not respond to a request for comment.
 Meanwhile, activity by the group’s members appears to have waned. The Intercept found just 144,000 members marked “active” in the online data, versus claims on the TPP website of a “ network of 3 million activists,” of “ more than 3 million supporters,” and of “over 3 million patriots.” Data from local chapters show members are clustered in fast-growing areas like Colorado and all along the Sun Belt, from California through Arizona, Texas, Georgia, and Florida.
  The 327 gigabytes of TPP data were provided to The Intercept anonymously by a source who claimed to have hacked the group’s web back end. In January, The Intercept  obtained documents that exposed the identity of a handful of wealthy TPP donors, but the new data fleshes out the understanding of the group’s big-money backing.
 The data includes a trove of information about people who are members of Tea Party Patriots local chapters, have signed petitions, or have donated: their names, phone numbers, home addresses, and a detailed activity history for each user. The Intercept is not naming or otherwise exposing information on individual members of the organization other than the group’s three biggest donors (at least two of whom were reported billionaires).
 Because the data obtained by the hacker comes only from the group’s web infrastructure, there could be important records missing. For example, there might be TPP supporters who signed up at a live event or made in-person donations but are not tracked by the web database. Some of the data provided by the hacker was corroborated with publicly available information, including some donations and TPP petitions. Still, it’s impossible to authenticate all of the data, and after The Intercept obtained the data a hacker altered pages on TPP’s website.
 TPP did not answer specific questions about the breach but instead  provided The Intercept an email sent to members from co-founder Jenny Beth Martin, notifying them of the hack and adding that the group had contacted law enforcement and worked “to ensure that our systems are not compromised and are secured even further to ensure that an event of this type does not happen again.” The email continued, “And you can be certain that we will take every step possible to find and help prosecute these criminals who have broken into our electronic home and stolen proprietary and confidential information.”
  TPP was founded in 2009, shortly after the inauguration of President Barack Obama. The group,  according to  numerous accounts, was  inspired by an on-air rant by CNBC editor Rick Santelli against an Obama administration  proposal to help homeowners avoid foreclosure in the early days of the financial crisis. TPP spent its first years organizing against the Affordable Care Act and government spending in general; today, reining in federal expenditures remains central to the group’s  stated priorities. But  racial and  anti-immigrant animus has  regularly appeared within the  group, which was also  involved in organizing the “March to Save America” rally culminating in the deadly January 6, 2021, storming of the U.S. Capitol, aimed at preventing Congress from certifying Joe Biden’s electoral victory. (TPP has said it did not fund the rally and  stated it was “shocked, outraged, and saddened at the turn of events on January 6,” condemning the violence.)
  Records from the hacked database shed light on its major backers. Now-deceased California real estate mogul Sanford Diller was another billionaire who provided major funding to TPP. According to tax documents, the TPP Foundation took in $106,318 in revenue in 2015. And according to the hacked data, they only took in two donations that year, and one of them was a $100,000 check from Diller. Diller donated another $100,000 in 2016, and $50,000 more in 2017, to the foundation. In 2016 he also donated $150,000 to TPP’s super PAC. The Intercept  reported on some of Diller’s foundation donations earlier this year, and late last year ABC News said Justice Department documents implicated Diller in a  secret lobbying scheme to trade political donations to entities associated with former President Donald Trump for a pardon.
 Another major funder of TPP is David Gore, an  Oregon libertarian whose family owns the Gore-Tex fabric company. Between 2018 and January 2021 he donated $50,000 to TPP Action, $275,000 to TPP’s super PAC, and $124,000 to TPP Foundation, according to the internal data obtained by The Intercept. Gore could not be reached for comment.
 Tea Party Patriots has three separate organizations: a 501(c)(3) public charity called TPP Foundation; a 501(c)(4) social welfare organization, which is allowed to engage in more extensive lobbying than a 501(c)(3), called TPP Action; and a super political action committee, which can spend unlimited amounts of dark money to support political candidates, called TPP Citizens Fund.
 The hacked data includes information about individual donations to these three organizations, but it doesn’t include money raised from interest groups and corporations. For example, TPP’s super PAC raised a total of $2.9 million to support Trump’s 2020 election campaign, but individual donor records from the hacked data only add up to $460,000 that election cycle.
 The hacked records also indicate that while TPP has cultivated the image of a mass movement, less than half a million people have either joined a local chapter or even just signed an online petition starting in 2013 or earlier. Of those members, roughly a third are marked “active.”
 The data describes roughly 800 local chapters, including a list of members for each chapter. Local TPP chapters have a total of 15,000 users who are marked active, meaning that only about 10 percent of active users in TPP’s database are members of a local chapter — everyone else are people who have signed petitions, donated, or subscribed to mailing lists.
  This map, based on the hacked data, shows how many active Tea Party Patriots users live in which cities, for cities that have at least 10 users.
title: 主要茶党集团得到亿万富翁捐助者的支持，被黑文件显示
title_en: Major Tea Party Group Was Backed by Billionaire Donors, Hacked Documents Reveal

一家科学期刊的编辑委员会的八名成员在发表了一系列有争议的论文后辞职，批评人士担心这些论文可能被用于 DNA 分析和对中国少数民族的迫害。该期刊，分子遗传学与基因组医学，是最新卷入涉及伦理问题研究的争议的期刊。 The Intercept 获得的电子邮件显示，该杂志的主编对有关这些论文的询问反应迟缓，这些论文涉及对藏族和维吾尔族等其他民族的研究，并于 3 月首次引起她的注意。该杂志由 Wiley 出版，Wiley 是一家总部位于新泽西的跨国公司，是世界一流的科学出版商之一。涉及 DNA 分析、面部识别和器官移植的研究在其他期刊上引发了争议，但这是第一次有如此多的期刊编辑委员会成员（25 人中有 8 人）因此类问题而辞职。比利时鲁汶大学的生物信息学家伊夫·莫罗 (Yves Moreau) 标记了这些论文，他在过去几年中发起了一场不知疲倦的运动，要求期刊撤回令人不安或不道德的论文。 Moreau 的探索始于 2015 年，当时科威特宣布计划强制收集所有公民、居民和游客的 DNA。他帮助带头发起了一场国际违法运动，并在次年被推翻时赢得了早期胜利。他开始相信，如果任其发展，科学和人工智能将被用来进一步推进威权主义。 “在技术方面，我们有一个非常舒适的极客形象，”他说。 “但当你真正审视技术的历史时，你会发现它一直是权力的纽带——至少有 2000 年了。”虽然许多遗传学家几十年来一直致力于推翻种族是一个科学概念的观点，但莫罗看到世界各地的当局可以利用新技术来获取政治利益，例如现成的 DNA 测试。莫罗后来将注意力转向了中国的 DNA 分析，特别是在新疆，估计有 100 万维吾尔人和其他少数民族被关押在集中营或被迫劳动。那里的当局还收集了居民的 DNA 样本。 Moreau 会定期自动搜索有关道德问题的论文。今年早些时候，该搜索在 Molecular Genetics & Genomic Medicine 上找到了 18 篇论文。一些论文描述了种族之间的遗传差异。警方可以将此类研究用于 DNA 分析，以更好地将犯罪嫌疑人与来自更广泛人群的 DNA 样本进行匹配。其他论文依赖于莫罗怀疑是在未经适当同意的情况下采集的样本。中国政府一直在收集各族男性的 DNA，旨在为中国所有 7 亿男性构建基因信息。中国警方还强行收集特定群体的 DNA，包括农民工和持不同政见者。
虽然 Molecular Genetics & Genomic Medicine 不是基因研究的主要出口，但它的影响因子为 2.183，这意味着它的论文被其他科学家引用和阅读。 Wiley 的名字赋予它一种受人尊敬的称号。正如其标题所暗示的那样，该杂志的成立旨在专注于医学应用的遗传学研究。许多编委会成员研究遗传学如何帮助医生治疗患者或帮助科学家治愈疾病。但在 2019 年，该杂志开始发表中国作者关于法医遗传学的论文，该领域涉及与警方的密切合作。法医遗传学在美国长期以来一直存在争议。在中国，问题更大，DNA 收集是持续迫害少数民族和其他群体的一部分。该杂志发表的一篇论文的标题是“新疆蒙古族群的法医特征和遗传亲和性分析，使用一种包括 41-Y-STR 和 3 个 Y-InDels 在内的新型六荧光染料标记分型系统”。另一幅图绘制了中国占多数的民族、汉族和其他群体（包括藏族和回族穆斯林）的分支之间的遗传差异。几篇论文列出了与中国警方有关联的机构的合著者或资助。其中之一列出了西藏公安局（该地区的警察机构）的合著者。今年 3 月，Moreau 在给美国国立卫生研究院国家人类基因组研究所医学遗传学和基因组医学培训项目的主编兼副主任苏珊娜·哈特 (Suzanne Hart) 的一封电子邮件中详细阐述了他的担忧。他指出，自2013年创刊以来，该杂志仅发表了两篇来自中国以外的法医遗传学研究。 “这表明 MGGM 已被明确确定为可以发表对弱势藏族和 [M]uslim 少数民族的法医人口遗传研究的期刊，”他写道。
note_en:
Eight members of the editorial board of a scientific journal have resigned after it published a slew of controversial papers that critics fear could be used for DNA profiling and persecution of ethnic minorities in China.
 The journal, Molecular Genetics & Genomic Medicine, is the latest to be caught up in controversy involving ethically fraught research. Emails obtained by The Intercept show that the journal’s editor-in-chief has been slow to respond to queries about the papers, which involve research on Tibetans and Uyghurs, among other ethnic groups, and were first brought to her attention in March. The journal is published by Wiley, a multinational company based in New Jersey that is one of the world’s premier scientific publishers.
 Studies involving DNA profiling, facial recognition, and organ transplantation have sparked controversy at other journals, but this is the first time that so many members of a journal’s editorial board — eight of 25 — have resigned in response to such issues.
  The papers were flagged by Yves Moreau, a bioinformatician at the University of Leuven in Belgium who over the past few years has waged a  tireless campaign to get journals to retract troubling or unethical papers.
 Moreau’s quest began in 2015, when Kuwait announced plans for compulsory collection of DNA from all citizens, residents, and visitors. He helped spearhead an international campaign against the law and won an early victory when it was  overturned the following year. He became convinced that if left unchecked, science and artificial intelligence would be used to further authoritarianism. “In technology, we have this nice comfortable geek image,” he said. “But when you really look at the history of technology, you see that it has been a nexus of power forever — for at least 2,000 years.” While many geneticists have worked for decades to overturn the idea that race is a scientific concept, Moreau saw that authorities around the world could exploit new technologies like readily available DNA testing for political gain.
  Moreau later turned his attention to DNA profiling in China, particularly in  Xinjiang, where an estimated 1 million Uyghurs and other ethnic minorities have been interned in camps or forced into labor. Authorities there have also  collected DNA samples from residents. Moreau periodically runs an automated search for papers on ethically charged topics. Earlier this year, that search turned up 18 papers at Molecular Genetics & Genomic Medicine.
 Some of the papers describe genetic differences between ethnic groups. Police can use such research for DNA profiling, to better match crime suspects with DNA samples from the broader population. Other papers relied on samples that Moreau suspected were taken without proper consent. The Chinese government has been collecting  DNA from men of all ethnicities, with the aim of building out genetic information for all 700 million males in China. Chinese police also  forcibly collect DNA from certain groups, including migrant workers and political dissidents.
 While Molecular Genetics & Genomic Medicine isn’t a leading outlet for genetic research, it has an impact factor of 2.183, meaning that its papers are cited and read by other scientists. The Wiley name lends it an imprimatur of respectability.
 As its title suggests, the journal was founded to focus on genetics research with medical applications. Many of the editorial board members study how genetics can help doctors treat patients or help scientists cure disease. But in 2019, the journal started publishing papers by authors in China on forensic genetics, a field that involves close collaboration with police. Forensic genetics has  long been  controversial in the  United States. It is even more problematic in China, where DNA collection is part of a sustained effort to persecute ethnic minorities and other groups.
 The title of  one paper published by the journal is “Forensic characteristics and genetic affinity analyses of Xinjiang Mongolian group using a novel six fluorescent dye-labeled typing system including 41-Y-STRs and 3 Y-InDels.”  Another maps genetic differences between branches of China’s majority ethnic group, Han Chinese, and other groups, including Tibetans and Hui Muslims. Several of the papers list co-authors or funding from institutions affiliated with Chinese police.  One lists a co-author from the Public Security Bureau in Tibet, the police agency in the region.
  In March, Moreau detailed his concerns in an email to Suzanne Hart, the journal’s editor-in-chief and deputy director at the medical genetics and genomic medicine training program with the U.S. National Institutes of Health’s National Human Genome Research Institute. He noted that since it was founded in 2013, the journal had published only two forensic genetic studies from outside China. “This suggests that MGGM has been specifically identified as a journal where forensic population genetic studies of vulnerable Tibetan and [M]uslim minorities can be published,” he wrote.
title: 科学杂志因充满伦理道德的中国遗传学论文而辞职
title_en: Resignations at Scientific Journal over Ethically Fraught China Genetics Papers

note:
Race Capital 的 Edith Yeung 帮助向 The Exchange 解释了 Deliveroo 的选择，称西班牙市场的人口并不多，这可能意味着“成为西班牙第一的潜在优势有[a] 上限”。虽然她指出她无法访问 Deliveroo 数据，但她的声明与该公司自己的评论相吻合，即西班牙在 2021 年上半年占其总交易额 (GTV) 不到 2%。市场没什么大不了的，但我们很好奇 Deliveroo 关于需要市场领导地位——或接近市场领导地位——以保证持续投资的评论。这是初创公司争夺市场地位的普遍现实，无论这些市场是城市还是国家？一些创业市场已经趋向于垄断或双头垄断。优步与滴滴在中国的战斗导致两家公司同意停止竞争。优步最近还将其在印度的 Uber Eats 业务出售给了 Zomato。在美国，Uber 和 Lyft 的较小竞争对手早已被遗忘，这两家美国叫车巨头仍在继续争夺主导地位。这种整合趋势还有其他熟悉的例子。送餐游戏集中在领先玩家中。 Postmates 作为一家独立公司未能幸存下来，最终成为 Uber 业务的一部分。也许 Gopuff 会设法在市场上占据一席之地，但根据 Second Measure 的数据，DoorDash 和 Uber Eats 在今年 6 月共占美国食品配送业务的 83%。一些初创市场倾向于垄断或双头垄断也就不足为奇了。许多国家通过专利保护知识产权，这些专利可以在很长一段时间内将新的创新限制在一个或两个参与者身上。当发明新技术或商业方法时，垄断也可能出现——例如，谷歌的互联网解析搜索技术导致了许多市场的近乎垄断。在规模效率有很大影响的企业中，当领先者整合较小的竞争对手直到只剩下一两家公司时，就会形成垄断。标准石油是此过程的典型示例。
按需交付市场的有趣之处在于，它既非常昂贵，又在技术上并不难进入，这意味着世界各地的许多公司都已进入该领域。这意味着按需交付与其他受专利保护的市场相反，我们可能期望从中形成垄断或竞争将在前两名参与者之后消失。然而，它也是一个规模经济可以在利润产生中发挥关键作用的行业，竞争加剧可能导致价格战和广告争夺战。这是一个成熟的整合市场，即使它缺乏可利用的 IP 基础。
note_en:
Race Capital’s  Edith Yeung helped explain the Deliveroo choice to The Exchange, saying the Spanish market doesn’t have a very large population, which may mean that the “potential upside for being #1 in Spain has [a] ceiling.”
 While she noted that she doesn’t have access to Deliveroo data, her statement jibes with the company’s own comment that Spain made up less than 2% of its aggregate gross transaction value (GTV) in the first half of 2021.
     One company exiting a market is not a big deal, but we were curious about Deliveroo’s comments regarding the need for market leadership — or something close to it — to warrant continued investment. Is this the common reality for startups battling for market position, no matter if those markets are cities or countries?
 Some startup markets have trended toward monopolies or duopolies. The Uber-Didi battle in China led to the companies agreeing to stop competing. Uber also  recently sold its Uber Eats business in India to Zomato. In the United States, Uber and Lyft’s smaller competitors have long been forgotten and both the American ride-hailing giants continue to battle for dominance.
 There are other familiar examples of this trend of consolidation. The food delivery game is concentrated amongst leading players. Postmates failed to survive as an independent company,  winding up as part of Uber’s operations. Perhaps Gopuff will manage to claw out a spot in the market, but DoorDash and Uber Eats together accounted for 83% of the U.S. food delivery business in June this year,  per Second Measure data.
 It’s no surprise that some startup markets lean toward monopolies or duopolies. Many countries protect intellectual property via patents that can constrain new innovation to one or two players for an extended period of time. Monopolies can also arise when a new technology or method of business is invented — Google’s internet parsing search tech led to a nigh-monopoly in many markets, for example.
 In businesses where efficiencies of scale have a large effect, monopolies can form when leading players consolidate smaller competitors until just one or two companies remain. Standard Oil is the canonical example of this process.
 What’s interesting about the on-demand delivery market is that it is both incredibly expensive but isn’t very technologically difficult to get into, which has meant that many companies have jumped into the sector around the world. This means on-demand delivery is the opposite of other patent-protected markets from which we might expect monopolies to form or competition to be extinguished past the top two players.
 Yet, it’s also an industry where economies of scale can play a key role in profit generation, and increased competition can lead to price wars and advertising tussles. It’s a ripe market, then, for consolidation, even if it lacks an exploitable IP base.
title: 铜牌对独角兽有意义吗？
title_en: Do bronze medals ever make sense for unicorns?

note:
虽然联邦政府没有强制要求企业提供疫苗接种证明，但一些城市正在制定自己的政策。最近，纽约市成为第一个要求为室内餐厅和体育馆提供疫苗接种证明的城市，而旧金山现在正在探索一系列类似的规定。未来其他城市可能会选择效仿。此外，美国各地的当地企业主都在联邦或州指导之外实施自己的措施，包括要求客户戴口罩或提供疫苗接种证明，或要求其员工接种疫苗。这些选择通常是有代价的，因为这些企业冒着社交媒体强烈反对和反疫苗人群负面评论的风险。该公司解释说，Yelp 的新功能将代表一种帮助减轻这种反应的尝试。 Yelp 表示，如果企业选择激活与 Covid 疫苗政策相关的两个新选项中的任何一个，它将主动利用自动化系统和人工审核员的组合来保护企业免受客户的攻击。尽管该公司早已建立了解决“审查轰炸”事件的系统，但 Yelp 表示，这种做法最近几个月变得更糟。过去，受到公众负面关注的企业可能会收到大量来自没有相关企业第一手经验的评论，这违反了 Yelp 的政策。然后，Yelp 可能会提醒访问该企业页面的访问者存在虚假评论的可能性，或者您的异常活动激增。该公司有时甚至会暂时阻止用户发表评论。在某些情况下，Yelp 还需要删除虚假评论或违反其政策的评论。但自 2021 年 1 月以来，Yelp 表示，它不得不在其页面上放置 100 多个异常活动警报，以响应一家因其 Covid 健康和安全实践而引起公众关注的企业。这包括企业是否通知客户其员工或顾客需要接种疫苗。
结果，Yelp 不得不删除近 4,500 条违反其内容指南的评论。由于 Yelp 已经在处理此类事件，因此现在更正式地为企业引入了一种通过新产品标记其 Covid 政策的方式。该公司指出，它在 2020 年 6 月推出了我们的黑人拥有的属性时建立了一个类似的系统，并通过主动监控业务再次对其他身份属性（例如拉丁裔拥有的、亚洲拥有的和 LGBTQ 拥有的）遵循相同的流程针对任何违反其内容准则的仇恨、种族主义或其他有害内容激活这些属性的页面。该公司告诉 TechCrunch，企业主也需要其新的 Covid 政策功能。 Yelp 用户运营副总裁 Noorie Malik 表示：“企业主和消费者都对 Yelp 发布疫苗相关属性表示了兴趣。 “几个月来，我们已经看到企业对其客户和员工实施疫苗要求。因此，我们还看到越来越多的评论关注人们对 Covid 疫苗接种的立场，而不是他们对业务的实际经验，”马利克指出。企业希望得到保证，当他们选择共享此信息时，他们的页面将受到更积极的监控以防止虚假评论。当然，Yelp 明白，如果允许其评论平台成为一个远离客户详细介绍他们的第一手体验的地方，它的整体服务将变得不那么有用。
“Yelp 一直是当地企业值得信赖的信息来源，帮助每天来到 Yelp 的数百万人做出明智的支出决定，”马利克说。 “重要的是，消费者在与企业打交道时拥有获取相关第一手信息的资源。您可能会争辩说，这在公共卫生危机期间更为重要，因此从相关的第一手消费者体验中得出的评论至关重要。”该功能现已推出，可在 Yelp for Business 帐户页面上找到。
note_en:
While there is no federal mandate for businesses to require proof of vaccination, some cities are introducing their own policies. Recently, New York City became the first to  require proof of vaccination for indoor restaurants and gyms, and, San Francisco is  now exploring a similar set of mandates. Other cities may choose to follow suit in the future.
 In addition,  local  business  owners across the U.S. are implementing their own measures outside of federal or state guidance, including requiring masks or proof of vaccination for customers, or requiring their staff to be vaccinated. These choices often come at price, as the businesses risk social media backlash and bad reviews from the anti-vaccine crowd.
 Yelp’s new features will represent an attempt to help mitigate that reaction, the company explains.
  Yelp says it will proactively leverage a combination of automated systems and human moderators to safeguard businesses from attacks from customers if a business opts to activate either of the two new options related to their Covid vaccine policies.
 Though the company has long since had  systems in place to address “review bombing” incidents, Yelp says the practice has gotten worse in recent months.
  In the past, businesses that gained negative public attention may have had an influx of reviews from those who didn’t have a first-hand experience with the business in question, which violates Yelp’s policy. Yelp may then alert visitor to the business’s page that there’s the potential for fake reviews or that thee had been spikes in unusual activity. The company will sometimes even temporarily block users from being able to leave reviews. And in some cases, Yelp will also need to  remove false reviews or those that otherwise violate its policies.
 But since January 2021, Yelp says it’s had to place over 100  Unusual Activity Alerts on its pages in response to a business gaining public attention for their Covid health and safety practices. This has included if a business notified customers that vaccinations were required for its employees or for its patrons.
 As a result, Yelp has had to remove nearly 4,500 reviews for violating its content guidelines.
  As Yelp was already handling these types of incidents, it’s now more formally introducing a way for businesses to flag their Covid policies through the new products.
 The company notes it put a similar system in place when it launched our  Black-owned attribute in June 2020 and again followed the same process for other identity attributes (e.g.  Latinx-owned,  Asian-owned, and  LGBTQ-owned) by proactively monitoring business pages that activated these attributes for any hateful, racist or other harmful content that violated its content guidelines.
 The company tells TechCrunch there was demand for its new Covid policy feature from business owners, as well.
  “Both business owners and consumers have expressed interest in Yelp releasing vaccine-related attributes,” said Noorie Malik, Yelp’s VP of User Operations. “For many months we’ve seen businesses implement vaccine requirements for both their customers and staff. As a result, we’ve also seen a rise in reviews focused on people’s stance on Covid vaccinations rather than their actual experience with the business,” Malik noted.
 The businesses want to be assured that their page will be more actively monitored for false reviews when they choose to share this information.
 Yelp, of course, understands that if allowed its reviews platform to become a place that veered away from customers detailing their first-hand experiences, it service overall would become less useful.
 “Yelp has always served as a trusted source of information on local businesses, helping the millions of people that come to Yelp every day make informed spending decisions,” Malik said. “It’s important that consumers have a resource for relevant first-hand information when engaging with a business. You could argue this is even more important during a public health crisis, making reviews from relevant first-hand consumer experiences critical.”
 The feature is rolling out now and can be found on the  Yelp for Business account page.
title: Yelp 添加了工具，让企业可以分享与疫苗相关的 Covid 政策
title_en: Yelp adds tools that let businesses share their Covid policies related to vaccines

note: 这家芯片制造商在一份声明中表示，高通以每股 37 美元的价格出价 46 亿美元，已经获得公司董事会的批准，不需要股东​​投票。 Veoneer 和 Magna 在 7 月份表示，两家公司的董事会都批准了此次收购。 Veoneer 是高级驾驶员辅助系统、决策车辆硬件和软件的开发商，可以在特定条件下执行一组有限的操作，例如在高速公路上变换车道或紧急制动。尽管 ADAS 与“自动驾驶汽车”相去甚远，但它已成为一种流行且可实现的功能，这些功能出现在当今提供的更多新车中。麦格纳和高通之间迅速展开的竞购战看好 ADAS 技术的未来，因为每家公司都希望与一级 ADAS 供应商大陆和博世保持竞争力。高通的市值目前为 1648 亿美元，而麦格纳的市值为 253 亿美元。目前尚不清楚麦格纳是否会提交反投标。市场对新出价做出了回应，Veoneer 的股价在周三和周四之间上涨了近 7 美元，从 31.22 美元上涨至 38.20 美元。在 7 月 23 日宣布出售给 Magna 之前，Veoneer 的股价为每股 19.93 美元。 Veoneer 于 2018 年从汽车安全系统制造商 Autoliv 分拆出来。 该公司与沃尔沃汽车成立了一家名为 Zenuity 的合资企业，专注于先进的驾驶辅助系统。该合资企业后来于去年 7 月分拆。 ADAS 开发商还与高通公司有预先存在的关系。两家公司在一月份签署了一项协议，在 ADAS 平台上进行合作。
note_en:
Qualcomm’s $4.6 billion bid, which comes in at $37 per share, has already received approval from the company’s board and would not need a stockholder vote, the chipmaker said in a statement. Veoneer and Magna said in July that both companies’ boards had approved the acquisition.
 Veoneer is a developer of advanced driver assistance systems, decision-making vehicle hardware and software that can perform a limited set of actions under certain conditions, like changing lanes on a highway or emergency braking. While ADAS is a far cry from “self-driving cars,” it has become a popular — and attainable — set of features that are appearing in a greater share of new vehicles on offer today.
 The burgeoning bidding war between Magna and Qualcomm is bullish on the future of ADAS technology, as each company seeks to stay competitive with Tier-1 ADAS suppliers Continental and Bosch. Qualcomm’s market capitalization currently sits at $164.8 billion, while Magna’s is $25.3 billion. It’s unclear whether Magna will submit a counter-bid.
 The market has responded to the new bid, with the stock price of Veoneer rising nearly $7 per share, from $31.22 to $38.20 between Wednesday and Thursday. Prior to the announcement of the sale to Magna on July 23, Veoneer stock sat at $19.93 per share.
 Veoneer was spun off from automotive safety system maker Autoliv in 2018. The company formed a joint venture with Volvo Cars, dubbed Zenuity, that was focused on advanced driver assistance systems. That venture was later split last July.
 The ADAS developer also has a pre-existing relationship with Qualcomm. The two companies signed an agreement in January to collaborate on an ADAS platform.
title: 高通希望以 4.6B 美元的价格购买 Veoneer，击败麦格纳的报价
title_en: Qualcomm wants to buy Veoneer for $4.6B, beating Magna’s offer

note:
据高级政府官员称，拜登总统希望到 2030 年在美国销售的所有新车中有 50% 是全电动、插电式混合动力车或氢动力汽车——他将在今天晚些时候的行政命令中提出这一目标.此外，他的政府将提出新的燃油经济性和排放标准，这或多或少会抹去特朗普政府对奥巴马时代之前涵盖到 2025 年制造的汽车的规定的回滚。拜登还将签署一项行政命令，责成环境保护局（ EPA) 和美国国家公路交通安全管理局 (NHTSA) 制定积极的长期规则来支持他的 2030 年目标，其中包括中型和重型车辆。 “当我说电动汽车是未来时，我不是在开玩笑，”周三晚上总统在推特上写道。当我说电动汽车是未来时，我不是在开玩笑。关注明天的大新闻。 pic.twitter.com/riIClqPYnw — 拜登总统 (@POTUS) 2021 年 8 月 5 日计划从内燃机转向这一计划并不像世界各地提出或启动的方法那样激进。欧盟已提议到 2035 年实际上禁止销售新型燃气动力乘用车，但法国已推迟淘汰仍使用化石燃料的混合动力车。英国希望到 2030 年停止销售新的内燃机汽车。中国希望在 2035 年销售的所有新车至少是混合动力车，但目标是 50% 是插电式混合动力车、电池电动车或氢动力车.加利福尼亚州一直是州级环境法规的领导者，它也希望到 2035 年禁止使用新的汽油车。
在政策方面，据路透社报道，美国国家公路交通安全管理局 (NHTSA) 的拟议规则将锁定年均燃油经济性增长略低于 4%，以及 2026 款车型的最终全车队平均每加仑约 52 英里.根据奥巴马时代的规定，汽车制造商必须每年将其车队的平均燃油经济性提高 5%，目标是在 2026 款车型中达到每加仑 54 英里的车队平均燃油经济性。在气候危机和呼吸道病毒大流行期间，特朗普政府大幅降低了这一比例，每年仅增加 1.5%，目标平均为每加仑 40 英里。尽管美国环保署此前认定汽车制造商“过度遵守”奥巴马时代的规定，但它还是这样做了。与此同时，EPA 将提出一项规则，要求根据拟议的燃油经济性收益进行减排，因为燃油消耗和尾气排放具有内在联系。在最终规则被提出和接受之前，这两项规则提案现在将经过长达数月的公众意见征询过程。拜登政府的新提案已经获得了一些主要汽车制造商的支持，它们将在今天晚些时候分别宣布自己的车队电气化目标。通用汽车、福特和 Stellantis（菲亚特克莱斯勒与法国 PSA 集团合并时创建的公司）的代表将与拜登一起出席宣布行政命令和拟议规则，美国汽车工人联合会的领导人也将出席。也就是说，正如路透社报道的那样，这三家底特律汽车制造商只承诺到 2030 年 40% 到 50% 的新车销售是插电式汽车的“共同愿望”。他们还要求政府大力投入时间，不仅是对新电动汽车的激励，还有“扩大美国电动汽车制造和供应链的激励”，根据一份新闻稿。拜登政府一直在推动在两党基础设施​​法案中支持电动汽车的采用，尽管一些拟议的投资（如充电站）已经在谈判中被削减。通用汽车曾表示，它希望到 2040 年实现碳中和，到 2035 年消除汽车尾气排放只是一个“愿望”。福特在 Mustang Mach-E 上取得了早期成功，并承诺到 2030 年在欧洲实现全电动。这家汽车制造商计划到当年在全球范围内使 40% 的车队实现全电动。 Stellantis 此前曾表示，到 2030 年，其北美销售额的 40% 以上将是插电式汽车。虽然底特律汽车制造商已经宣布对电动汽车进行大规模投资，但在特朗普政府期间，他们在政策上肯定没有那么团结。在 EPA 和 NHTSA 在特朗普领导下取消奥巴马时代的标准后，福特（以及大众、本田、宝马和沃尔沃）与加利福尼亚达成协议，继续瞄准一个相对严格的目标。与此同时，通用汽车和当时仍然是菲亚特克莱斯勒的公司实际上加入了特朗普政府在法庭上就加利福尼亚制定自己的排放标准的权利的斗争。汽车制造商（以及丰田）坚持认为他们加入了法庭竞标，因为他们认为应该由联邦政府来制定这些类型的标准。但此后他们放弃了这场斗争——通用汽车在 2020 年大选之后，以及今年早些时候的 Stellantis。与此同时，拜登的环保署已经采取行动恢复加州的排放监管权力。
通用汽车在电动汽车上投资了数十亿美元，但在去年大选前与特朗普站在了一边 全电动汽车目前仅占美国总销量的 2% 左右，尽管随着新车和二手车市场的增长，它们在最近几个月大幅上涨红热。根据凯利蓝皮书的数据，2021 年第二季度在美国销售了超过 100,000 辆全电动汽车，这在很大程度上是由于特斯拉 Model 3 轿车和 Model Y SUV 的成功。但混合动力汽车的销量也有所增长，销量超过 250,000 辆——同比增长 200%。在某些方面，特斯拉已经是拜登政府希望其他人发展成为的那种汽车制造商。特斯拉只生产电动汽车，而首席执行官埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 对垂直整合的偏爱意味着特斯拉的汽车是由大量美国制造的零件制成的。但该公司不会参加周四的活动。 “特斯拉没有被邀请[S]看起来很奇怪，”马斯克周四早些时候在推特上写道。不过，拜登周四可能会谈到希望工会工人制造电动汽车，而特斯拉则以抵制工会努力而闻名。其他全电动汽车初创公司正在向没有工会劳动力的生产迈进，尽管 UAW 表示已经计划尝试改变这种状况。美国东部时间 8 月 5 日上午 8 点 30 分更新：添加了有关 Elon Musk 的推文和其他 EV 初创公司的信息。
note_en:
President Biden wants 50 percent of all new cars sold in the United States in 2030 to be all-electric, plug-in hybrid, or hydrogen-powered — a goal he will lay out in an executive order later today, according to senior administration officials. In addition, his administration will propose new fuel economy and emissions standards that will more or less erase the Trump administration’s  rollback of the previous Obama-era rules covering cars made through 2025.
 Biden will also sign an executive order that tasks the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) to develop aggressive long-term rules to support his 2030 target, ones that include medium- and heavy-duty vehicles as well.
 “When I say electric vehicles are the future, I’m not joking,” read a  tweet from the President on Wednesday night.
 When I say electric vehicles are the future, I’m not joking. Tune in for big news tomorrow.  pic.twitter.com/riIClqPYnw
— President Biden (@POTUS)  August 5, 2021
 This planned shift away from internal combustion engines is not as aggressive as the approaches that have been proposed or set in motion around the world. The European Union has proposed  a de-facto ban on sales of new gas-powered passenger vehicles by 2035, though France has pushed back on the phaseout of hybrids, which still use fossil fuels. The United Kingdom wants to  stop selling new combustion engine vehicles by 2030. China  wants all new cars sold in 2035 to be hybrids at the very least, but is aiming for 50 percent to be plug-in hybrid, battery electric, or hydrogen-powered.
 California, ever the leader on state-level environmental regulations, is also  looking to ban new gas cars by 2035.
 On the policy side, the proposed rule from the NHTSA will look to lock in average annual fuel economy increases of just under four percent, according to   Reuters, and an ultimate fleet-wide average of around 52 miles per gallon for 2026 model-year vehicles. Under the Obama-era rule, automakers had to increase the average fuel economy of their fleets by five percent each year, with the goal of hitting a fleet average of 54 miles per gallon in the 2026 model-year cars. The Trump administration  lowered this dramatically — during a climate crisis and a respiratory virus pandemic, no less — to yearly increases of just 1.5 percent and a target average of 40 miles per gallon. It did this despite the EPA previously determining that automakers were “ over-complying” with the Obama-era rule.
 The EPA, meanwhile, will propose a rule that requires emissions reductions that scale with the proposed fuel economy gains, as fuel consumption and tailpipe emissions are intrinsically linked. The two rule proposals will now go through a months-long public comment process before final rules are proposed and accepted.
  The Biden administration’s new proposals have already won support from some major automakers, which will separately announce their own goals for electrifying their fleets later today. Representatives from General Motors, Ford, and Stellantis (the company that was created when Fiat Chrysler merged with France’s PSA group) will appear alongside Biden as he announces the executive orders and proposed rules, as will leaders of the United Auto Workers union.
 That said, as was reported by   Reuters , those three Detroit automakers are only committing to a “shared aspiration” of 40 to 50 percent of new car sales being plug-ins by 2030. They’re also asking for the government to pitch in big time, not just on incentives for new electric vehicles, but also “incentives to expand the electric vehicle manufacturing and supply chains in the United States,” according to a press release. The Biden administration has been pushing to support electric vehicle adoption in  the bipartisan infrastructure bill, though some proposed investments (like for charging stations) have already been cut back in negotiations.
 GM has said it wants to  be carbon neutral by 2040 and that eliminating tailpipe emissions from its vehicles by 2035 is merely an “aspiration.” Ford, which has found early success with the Mustang Mach-E, has committed to going all-electric by 2030 —  in Europe. The automaker plans to make  40 percent of its fleet all-electric by that year globally. Stellantis had previously said more than 40 percent of its North American sales in 2030  would be plug-in vehicles.
 While the Detroit automakers have announced massive investments in electric vehicles, they were certainly not as united on policy during the Trump administration.
 After the EPA and NHTSA moved to roll back the Obama-era standards under Trump, Ford (as well as Volkswagen, Honda, BMW, and Volvo) struck a deal with California to  keep aiming at a relatively strict target. GM and what was still Fiat Chrysler at the time, meanwhile, actually joined the Trump administration’s fight in court over California’s right to set its own emissions standards. The automakers (along with Toyota) have maintained that they joined the court bid because they believed it should be up to the federal government to set these types of standards. But they have since abandoned that fight — GM  just after the 2020 election, and  Stellantis earlier this year. Meanwhile, Biden’s EPA has  already moved to restore California’s emissions regulation authority.
 GM is investing billions in EVs but sided with Trump before last year’s election
 All-electric vehicles currently only make up about  two percent of total sales in the US, though they’ve jumped sharply in recent months as the new and used car markets have  gotten red hot. More than 100,000 fully electric vehicles were sold in the US in the second quarter of 2021, according to  Kelley Blue Book, driven in large part by the  success of Tesla’s Model 3 sedan and Model Y SUV. But hybrid vehicle sales were up, too, with more than 250,000 sold — a 200-percent increase year over year.
 In some respects, Tesla is already the kind of automaker that the Biden administration wants others to evolve into. Tesla only makes electric vehicles, and CEO Elon Musk’s penchant for vertical integration means Tesla’s cars are built out of an  exceptionally high amount of American-made parts. But the company won’t be part of Thursday’s event. “[S]eems odd that Tesla wasn’t invited,” Musk  tweeted early Thursday.
 Biden is likely to speak Thursday about wanting electric vehicles to be made by union workers, though, and Tesla has  famously pushed back on unionization efforts. Other all-electric vehicle startups are moving forward towards production without union workforces, though the  UAW has said it’s already planning to try to change that.
 Update August 5th, 8:30AM ET:  Added info about Elon Musk’s tweet and other EV startups.
title: 拜登希望 2030 年销售的新车中有一半是混合动力或全电动
title_en: Biden Wants Half of New Cars Sold in 2030 To Be Hybrid or All-Electric

note: 由于太空舱推进剂系统中的阀门出现问题，波音公司的 Starliner 乘员舱在飞往国际空间站的无人试飞中于周二被取消。如果问题能够及时解决，任务管理人员可以选择在美国东部时间星期三下午 12 点 57 分再次尝试，如果天气允许的话。联合发射联盟阿特拉斯 5 火箭最初计划于上周五发射，但美国宇航局下令推迟，而俄罗斯空间站工程师解决了新抵达实验室模块的问题。上周末，Starliner 的发射被重置为周二。监测佛罗里达夏季午后通常暴风雨天气的预报员预测，条件可接受的可能性为 60%，然后将几率降低至 50-50。该团队继续加油，但在上午 10 点 30 分左右，波音公司宣布取消发射，并在推特上说：“我们确认今天的#Starliner Orbital Flight Test-2 发射已被取消。很快就会有更多细节。” Starliner 飞行标志着波音和美国宇航局的一个重要里程碑，因为该机构从搭乘俄罗斯联盟号飞船过渡到部署由波音和 SpaceX 建造的商业载人飞船。根据美国宇航局 26 亿美元的合同，SpaceX 于 2019 年成功发射了载人龙飞船，并于 2019 年成功进行了无人驾驶试飞，并于去年进行了试飞。从那时起，这家加利福尼亚火箭制造商向空间站发射了两次运营航班，将两名长期工作人员运送到前哨。根据一份价值 42 亿美元的合同，波音公司于 2019 年 12 月 20 日在无人试飞中发射了 Starliner，但与空间站对接的计划因软件问题而脱轨。公司经理指望本周的第二次试飞最终为飞行员任务扫清道路。根据吸取的经验教训，波音和 NASA 可能准备在今年年底或明年初将三名宇航员送上不同的 Starliner 太空舱。
note_en:
The launch of   Boeing's Starliner crew capsule on an unpiloted test flight to the International Space Station was scrubbed Tuesday because of problems with valves in the capsule's propellant system. Mission managers held open the option of making another attempt Wednesday at 12:57 p.m. ET, weather permitting, if the problem can be resolved in time.
 The launching atop a United Launch Alliance Atlas 5 rocket initially was planned for last Friday, but NASA ordered a delay while Russian space station engineers   resolved problems with a newly arrived laboratory module. Over the weekend, the Starliner launch was reset for Tuesday.
 Forecasters monitoring Florida's typically stormy summer afternoon weather predicted a 60% chance of acceptable conditions then lowered the odds to 50-50. The team pressed ahead with fueling, but around 10:30 a.m., Boeing announced the launch had been called off,  tweeting, "We're confirming today's #Starliner Orbital Flight Test-2 launch is scrubbed. More details soon."
 The Starliner flight marks a major milestone for Boeing and NASA as the agency transitions from hitching rides aboard Russian Soyuz spacecraft to fielding commercial crew ships built by Boeing and SpaceX.
 SpaceX, under a $2.6 billion NASA contract, launched its Crew Dragon spacecraft on a successful unpiloted test flight in 2019 and a piloted test flight last year. Since then, the California rocket builder has launched two operational flights to the space station carrying two long-duration crews to the outpost.
 Boeing, under a $4.2 billion contract, launched its Starliner on an unpiloted test flight December 20, 2019, but plans to dock with the space station were   derailed by software problems. Company managers are counting on this week's second test flight to finally clear the way for piloted missions.
 Depending on lessons learned, Boeing and NASA could be ready to launch three astronauts aboard a different Starliner capsule by the end of this year or early next year.
title: 波音公司将 Starliner 乘员舱发射到空间站
title_en: Boeing Scrubs Launch of Starliner Crew Capsule To Space Station

note: 微软已通知员工，从 9 月开始，任何进入美国微软大楼的人都需要提供疫苗接种证明。因身体状况或受保护原因（例如宗教信仰）而无法接种疫苗的员工可以获得特殊住宿。该公司还将办公室的全面重新开放日期从 9 月推迟到“不早于 2021 年 10 月 4 日”。正如我们自大流行开始以来所做的那样，我们将继续密切跟踪新的发展并随着这种情况的发展调整我们的计划，将员工的健康和安全放在首位。根据我们与健康和数据专家的持续协商，我们在美国全面开放工作场所的最早日期不早于 2021 年 10 月 4 日。从 9 月开始，我们还将要求所有员工、供应商、以及任何进入美国 Microsoft 大楼的客人，并将为员工制定适当的住宿流程。我们将继续在我们工作的每个地区/国家/州的地方基础上审查情况，并将根据需要调整日期和政策。微软告诉 The Verge，那些照顾免疫抑制患者的工作人员，或者是孩子太小而无法接种疫苗的父母，将能够在家工作到 1 月份。微软的公告是在全国范围内 COVID-19 案件激增以及其他科技公司实施更严格的政策之后发布的。 Facebook 要求所有美国员工在办公室戴口罩，并要求所有员工在返回办公室时接种 COVID-19 疫苗。 Facebook 预计将在 10 月全面重新开放。谷歌还将要求员工在返回办公室之前接种疫苗，并将返回时间推迟到 10 月 18 日。
note_en:
Microsoft has informed employees that it will require proof of vaccination for anyone entering a Microsoft building in the US starting in September. Employees who have a medical condition or a protected reason, such as religion, that prevents them from getting a vaccine can get a special accommodation. The company has also pushed its full office reopening date from September to “no earlier than October 4th, 2021.”
  As we have done since the beginning of the pandemic, we continue to closely track new developments and adapt our plans as this situation evolves, keeping employee health and safety top of mind. Based on our continued consultation with health and data experts, our earliest date for the full opening of our U.S. worksites will be no earlier than October 4, 2021. Starting in September, we’ll also require proof of vaccination for all employees, vendors, and any guests entering Microsoft buildings in the U.S., and will have an accommodation process in place for employees. We continue to review the situation on a local basis in each region/country/state where we work and will adjust dates and policies as needed.
 Staff who are caregivers to immunosuppressed people or are parents with children too young to get the vaccine will be able to work from home until January, Microsoft tells  The Verge.
 Microsoft’s announcements follow a surge in COVID-19 cases around the country and stricter policies put in place by other tech companies.  Facebook is requiring all US employees to wear masks in offices and that  all employees are vaccinated against COVID-19 when they return to offices. Facebook expects to  fully reopen in October. Google will also  require employees to be vaccinated before they can return to the office and has delayed that return to October 18th.
title: 微软将需要 COVID-19 疫苗接种证明才能进入美国的建筑物
title_en: Microsoft Will Require Proof of COVID-19 Vaccination To Enter Buildings in the US

note:
• 这是有史以来第一次，即 2021 年 6 月，小米成为全球智能手机月度销量（销量）第一品牌 • 虽然这对小米来说是一项伟大的壮举，但一旦三星的生产恢复，这可能只是暂时的来自越南的 COVID-19 浪潮，但根据 Counterpoint Research 的每月市场脉搏服务的数据，小米在 2021 年 6 月超越三星和苹果，首次成为全球第一大智能手机品牌。 2021 年 6 月，小米的销售额环比增长 26%，成为当月增长最快的品牌。就销量而言，小米还是 2021 年第二季度全球第二大品牌，自 2011 年成立以来，智能手机累计销量接近 8 亿部。在评论导致小米获得领先地位的市场动态时，研究总监塔伦·帕塔克 (Tarun Pathak) 指出，“自从华为开始走下坡路，小米就一直在不断地积极努力，以填补这次衰退造成的空白。 OEM 一直在扩展华为和 HONOR 的传统市场，如中国、欧洲、中东和非洲。 6月份，中国、欧洲和印度的复苏以及三星因供应受限而下滑，进一步帮助了小米。”在谈到中国​​市场和三星供应限制时，高级分析师 Varun Mishra 表示：“在 618 节的推动下，中国市场 6 月环比增长 16%，其中小米成为增长最快的 OEM，得益于其在低线城市的积极线下扩张以及红米 9、红米 Note 9 和红米 K 系列的稳定表现。与此同时，由于越南新一波 COVID-19 大流行，三星的生产在 6 月中断，导致该品牌的设备面临跨渠道短缺。凭借强大的中端产品组合和广泛的市场覆盖面，小米是三星 A 系列留下的短期缺口的最大受益者。”展望未来，如果越南的情况没有改善，随着小米继续从韩国品牌那里获得份额，三星的生产可能会继续受到打击。不过，一旦三星复苏，队伍很可能再次洗牌。您还可以访问我们的数据部分（每季度更新），查看全球、美国、中国和印度的智能手机市场份额。
Counterpoint Technology Market Research 是一家全球研究公司，专门研究 TMT（技术、媒体和电信）行业的产品。它为主要的技术和金融公司提供月度报告、定制项目以及对移动和技术市场的详细分析。其主要分析师都是高科技行业经验丰富的专家。
note_en:
• This is the first time ever, in June 2021, Xiaomi has become the number one brand in global monthly smartphone sales (sell-through) volumes
 • While this is a great feat for Xiaomi, it could prove to be temporary once Samsung’s production recovers from Vietnam’s COVID-19 wave, but the race has gotten close
  Xiaomi surpassed Samsung and Apple in June 2021 to become the number one smartphone brand in the world for the first time ever, according to Counterpoint Research’s Monthly  Market Pulse Service. Xiaomi’s sales grew 26% MoM in June 2021, making it the fastest-growing brand for the month. Xiaomi was also the  number two brand globally for Q2 2021 in terms of sales, and cumulatively, has sold close to 800 million smartphones since its inception in 2011.
    Commenting on the market dynamics that led to Xiaomi attaining pole position,  Research  Director Tarun Pathak noted, “Ever since the decline of Huawei commenced, Xiaomi has been making consistent and aggressive efforts to fill the gap created by this decline. The OEM has been expanding in  Huawei’s and HONOR’s legacy markets like  China,  Europe,  Middle East and Africa. In June, Xiaomi was further helped by China, Europe and  India’s recovery and Samsung’s decline due to supply constraints.”
 Commenting on the China market and Samsung supply constraints,  Senior Analyst Varun Mishra said, “China’s market grew 16% MoM in June driven by the  618 festival, with Xiaomi being the fastest growing OEM, riding on its aggressive offline expansion in lower-tier cities and solid performance of its Redmi 9, Redmi Note 9 and the Redmi K series. At the same time, due to a fresh wave of the COVID-19 pandemic in  Vietnam, Samsung’s production was disrupted in June, which resulted in the brand’s devices facing shortages across channels. Xiaomi, with its strong mid-range portfolio and wide market coverage, was the biggest beneficiary from the short-term gap left by Samsung’s A series.”
 Going forward, if the situation in  Vietnam does not improve, Samsung’s production is likely to remain hit with Xiaomi continuing to gain share from the Korean brand. However, once Samsung recovers, the ranks are likely to shuffle again.

Its key analysts are seasoned experts in the high-tech industry.

note_en:
Verizon Communications  is enlisting artificial intelligence models to help place thousands of 5G wireless transmitters for optimal performance.
  Later this year, the company will begin a multibillion-dollar rollout of midband spectrum, which expands coverage of its existing ultra wideband 5G wireless service.
  Maximizing coverage with the least number of transmitters is a priority, said Shankar Arumugavelu, senior vice president and global chief information officer of Verizon.
  “When we build out these networks, these are very capital-intensive,” he said. “We have to make sure that we are being very judicious in terms of how we are investing our capital.”
    The models, designed by in-house data scientists and other employees, factor in a number of variables that can alter the strength of 5G signals, like buildings, bridges, terrain, the position of the transmitter, as well as other transmitters nearby.
    Verizon, along with rivals  AT&T  and T-Mobile US Inc., is racing to build out nationwide 5G service,  a yearslong effort slowed by the lack of available airwaves for fast transmission and long signal ranges, and by the deployment of new network equipment, analysts have said.
  The largest U.S. carrier, Verizon began using AI models in 2018 to determine the best spots to install transmitters for its ultra wideband spectrum and is using AI again for its midband spectrum, considered the sweet spot in terms of range and speed. Verizon expects to cover 100 million people with midband spectrum by March 2022, according to the company.
  “Without AI, we would not be able to do this. Period,” Mr. Arumugavelu said.
  In a U.S. government airwaves auction earlier this year, Verizon  landed more than half of the midband spectrum.
  The carrier will pay  more than $50 billion for those airwaves and related expenses and is planning a total of $10 billion in capital expenditures over the next three years to expand its midband range, the WSJ has previously reported.
      T-Mobile, which is also bringing out its midband 5G network, has used AI models to help engineers prioritize the locations for upgrading its 4G gear to 5G, said Grant Castle, vice president of device engineering and technology labs.
  So-called small cell 5G transmitters, or antennas, are about the size of a backpack and are placed on vertical structures like utility poles or buildings.
  Verizon’s AI system can point to the best such perches by weighing various factors within specific ZIP Codes and how well the signal could cover the area, Mr. Arumugavelu said. Using computer-vision technology, the models can analyze spatial and geographic elements—bridges, trees and building heights around specific intersections, for instance—and find the most viable spot for a 5G transmitter, he added.
  The technology can also recommend the best positioning for the antennas so that the signal covers the area without causing interference with other antennas nearby, he said.
  Billions of dollars are at stake in the rollout of midband 5G spectrum, which is why Verizon is relying on AI, said Sid Nag, vice president of research for cloud and edge computing at technology research firm  Gartner
  “They have to be very precise and clinical to identify the places where they are going to place the nodes,” he said.
  Businesses are expected to adopt 5G faster than consumers, partly because of the technology’s potential to enable connectivity for drones, autonomous vehicles and remote healthcare, Mr. Nag said.
  Verizon and other carriers are also focused on making sure the 5G nodes are in just the right spots because they want their customers to be as satisfied as possible with the service, he said.

ReversingLabs 是一家总部位于马萨诸塞州剑桥的网络安全公司，开发威胁检测和分析解决方案，已在由 Crosspoint Capital Partners 牵头，ForgePoint Capital 和 Prelude 参与的 B 轮融资中筹集了 5600 万美元。联合创始人兼首席执行官 Mario Vuksan 表示，随着 ReversingLabs 希望扩大其全球影响力，所得收益将用于扩大 ReversingLabs 的销售和营销工作，使其总融资额达到 8100 万美元。在过去的一年中，发生了几起备受瞩目的攻击者试图通过软件供应链危害企业的事件。根据 Anchore 最近的一项调查，64% 的公司在 2021 年受到供应链攻击的影响，60% 已将保护软件供应链作为 2022 年的首要任务。这些攻击强调了对控制的需求，这些控制可以在开发、部署和采用生命周期中帮助验证软件及其组件的完整性。 ReversingLabs 由 Mario Vuksan 和 Tomislav Pericin 于 2009 年创立，旨在通过静态分析和文件信誉服务来应对不断增长的威胁，这些服务提供对恶意软件及其位置的可见性。该平台分析来自网络、移动、电子邮件、云和应用程序开发等垂直行业（如软件、金融服务、国防、零售和保险）中出现的基于文件和二进制的威胁。 CrossPoint 管理合伙人 Hugh Thompson 博士在一份新闻稿中表示：“当今网络安全攻击的复杂程度意味着企业不能再假设来自其供应商的软件产品是安全的。” “即使在您无法访问源代码的情况下，ReversingLabs 也提供了一种主动和透明的方法来了解软件中存在的威胁。” ReversingLabs 平台的核心是“Titanium”引擎，这是一个 AI 系统，可以收集数千种文件类型并持续监控超过 100 亿个文件的索引以应对未来威胁。该系统在底层对象结构中解压文件——下至嵌入的可执行文件、库、文档、资源和图标——并将“人类可读”的指标映射到分类。安全分析师获得威胁情报，他们可以让我们对威胁进行优先级排序，而威胁情报和狩猎团队获得一个工作台进行深度文件分析，表面上使他们能够加速调查。 “每个组织，无论是开发软件的集成软件供应商，还是采购或使用软件的企业，都需要控制来管理软件供应链攻击面，”Crosspoint 管理合伙人 Greg Clark 在一份声明中说。 “这个攻击面是微妙的，像源代码扫描这样的传统方法是不够的。代码、编译、构建和部署周期的每一部分都需要检查。 ReversingLabs 是对抗这些威胁的重要盟友。他们的解决方案是独一无二的，很难复制，而且非常有价值。”据 Gartner 称，ReversingLabs 参与的网络安全市场预计到 2022 年将达到 1704 亿美元。但该公司声称已经取得了进展，吸引了包括前六名软件公司中的四家和前五名国防和航空航天公司中的两家的客户。它还将 SolarWinds 算作合作伙伴，SolarWinds 是今年早些时候美国联邦政府广泛黑客攻击的中心的 IT 监控和管理公司。
SolarWinds 总裁兼首席执行官 Sudhakar Ramakrishna 在一份声明中表示：“作为我们 Secure By Design 计划的一个要素，我们已经尽最大努力保护我们的软件开发和部署管道的完整性，即使是最坚定和最老练的攻击者。” “我们正在努力帮助建立行业安全软件开发的新标准，ReversingLabs 已成为我们整体工作的重要组成部分。” VentureBeat 的使命是成为技术决策者的数字城镇广场，以获取有关变革性技术和交易的知识。我们的网站提供有关数据技术和策略的基本信息，以指导您领导您的组织。我们邀请您成为我们社区的一员，以访问：封闭的思想领袖内容和我们珍贵活动的折扣，例如 Transform 2021：了解更多 成为会员
note_en:
ReversingLabs, a Cambridge, Massachusetts-based cybersecurity company developing threat detection and analysis solutions, has  raised $56 million in series B funding led by Crosspoint Capital Partners with participation from ForgePoint Capital and Prelude. Cofounder and CEO Mario Vuksan says the proceeds, which bring its total raised to $81 million, will be put toward scaling ReversingLabs’ sales and marketing efforts as ReversingLabs looks to expand its global reach.
 Over the past year, there’s been several high-profile incidents where attackers have attempted to compromise enterprises through the software supply chain. According to a recent Anchore  survey, 64% of companies were affected by.a supply chain attack in 2021 and 60% have made securing the software supply chain a top 2022 priority. The attacks highlight the need for controls that can help validate the integrity of software and its components through the development, deployment, and adoption lifecycle.
  ReversingLabs, which was founded in 2009 by Mario Vuksan and Tomislav Pericin, aims to combat the growing threat with static analysis and file reputation services that provide visibility into malware and its location. The platform analyzes file and binary-based threats emerging from the web, mobile, email, cloud, and app development across industry verticals like software, financial services, defense, retail, and insurance.
 “The level of sophistication and complexity in today’s cybersecurity attacks means that enterprises can no longer assume that software products from their providers are safe,” CrossPoint managing partner Dr. Hugh Thompson said in a press release. “ReversingLabs provides a proactive and transparent approach to understanding the threats that exist within software even in cases where you don’t have access to source code.”
  At the core of the ReversingLabs platform is the “Titanium” engine, an  AI system that harvests thousands of file types and continuously monitors an index of over 10 billion files for future threats. The system unpacks files in the underlying object structure — down to embedded executables, libraries, documents, resources, and icons — and maps “human-readable” indicators to classifications. Security analysts get threat intelligence that they can us to prioritize threats, while threat intelligence and hunting teams get a workbench for deep file analysis, ostensibly enabling them to accelerate investigations.
 “Every organization, whether an integrated software vendor developing software or an enterprise procuring or using software, needs controls to manage the software supply chain attack surface,” Crosspoint managing partner Greg Clark said in a statement. “This attack surface is nuanced, and traditional approaches like source code scanning are insufficient. Every part of the code, compile, build and deploy cycle needs to be checked. ReversingLabs is a great ally in the fight against these threats. Their solution is unique, very hard to replicate and immensely valuable.”
 ReversingLabs competes in a cybersecurity market anticipated to be worth $170.4 billion in 2022,  according to Gartner. But the company claims to have made inroads, nabbing customers including four of the top six software companies and two of the top five defense and aerospace firms. It also counts  SolarWinds, the IT monitoring and management firm at the center of the widespread U.S. federal government hack earlier this year, as a partner.
 “As an element of our Secure By Design initiatives, we’ve applied maximum attention to protecting the integrity of our software development and deployment pipeline from even the most determined and sophisticated attackers,” SolarWinds president and CEO Sudhakar Ramakrishna said in a statement. “We are working to help establish new standards for secure software development in the industry and ReversingLabs has since become an important part of our overall efforts.”

Amazon told employees Thursday that it will delay its timeline for returning to regular work in the office until early next year.
 The new date: Jan. 3, 2022. Previously, Amazon had expected employees to start working in the office regularly the week of Sept. 7.
 However, Amazon stopped short of implementing a vaccine mandate for those who do work in the office, as companies including Microsoft, Google, and Facebook have done. Amazon said it requires employees to wear masks in the office, except for those who can verify they’ve been fully vaccinated.
  Amazon informed employees of the new timeline in a message this morning, and confirmed the details in a statement to GeekWire.
 Its decision ups the ante for corporate responses to the rise of the COVID-19 Delta variant. Microsoft earlier this week said the full reopening of its U.S. workplaces  will now take place no earlier than Oct. 4. Google and Apple currently have similar timelines.
 The role of Amazon and other tech giants as bellwethers for such decisions means that other companies often follow their lead.
 Amazon’s guidance for returning to work has evolved in response to changing public health guidelines and employee feedback.  The company  said in March that it planned to “return to an office-centric culture as our baseline.”
  It  relaxed that stance in June, saying it would offer “a mix of working between the office and home,” with a baseline of three days a week in the office.
 The seven-day rolling daily average of COVID-19 cases has more than quadrupled in Washington state in the past month,  to more than 1,500 daily cases as of Aug. 1. In King County, home to Amazon’s large operations in Seattle and Bellevue, 64% of the population is fully vaccinated, according to state data.

一项主要研究表明，饮酒会增加患癌症的风险，这比以前认为的要多，该研究还发现，喝咖啡可以预防肝癌。据伦敦帝国理工学院领导的一个国际团队称，饮酒与多种癌症有关，包括头颈部癌症——口腔癌、咽癌和喉癌——食道癌和肠癌，以及更广为人知的乳腺癌和肝癌。该研究还表明，每天至少喝一杯咖啡与患肝癌和皮肤基底细胞癌的风险较低有关，对含咖啡因和不含咖啡因的咖啡都有影响。资助这项研究的世界癌症研究基金会 (WCRF) 研究与创新主任乔塔·米特鲁 (Giota Mitrou) 说：“这项总体审查证实了我们对酒精和咖啡与癌症有关的证据。 “进一步的研究需要更好地了解咖啡与癌症之间以及酒精与不同癌症亚型之间的联系机制。”她说，担心自己的饮食如何影响患癌症风险的人可以参考 WCRF 的癌症预防建议，该建议提倡限制饮酒、保持健康的体重和身体活动，并享受富含全麦、蔬菜、水果和脉冲。酒精会增加患癌症的风险，因为当它被代谢时，它会分解成可以与 DNA 结合的化学物质，导致可能致癌的突变。
酒精还会增加与某些类型乳腺癌发展相关的激素水平。科学家们发现，一般来说，一个人饮用的酒精饮料越多，他们患上一种相关癌症的风险就越高。该研究的作者呼吁制定更有针对性的公共卫生政策，使人们能够了解饮酒与癌症之间的联系，以鼓励他们限制饮酒。相反，咖啡被认为具有抗氧化和抗炎特性，可以预防由炎症引发的疾病，如癌症。 Imperial 的研究查看了 860 篇已发表研究的评论数据，这些研究探讨了食物和营养摄入之间的关联，以及患上或死于 11 种不同癌症的风险。尽管研究人员发现癌症与大多数类型的食物和饮料之间存在薄弱联系，但对于他们研究的 11 种癌症，饮酒风险增加以及咖啡和乳制品风险降低的证据是强有力的。

Consuming alcohol increases the risk of getting more cancers than previously thought, according to a major study, which also found that drinking coffee protects against liver cancer.
 Alcohol consumption is linked to several cancers including those of the head and neck – mouth, pharynx and larynx – oesophageal and bowel, along with the more widely known connection with breast and liver cancer, according to an international team led by  Imperial College London.
  The study also showed that drinking at least one cup of coffee daily is associated with a lower risk of developing liver cancer and  basal cell carcinoma of the skin, with the effects observed for both caffeinated and decaffeinated coffee.
   Giota Mitrou, the director of research and innovation at World  Cancer Research Fund (WCRF), which funded the research, said: “This umbrella review confirms the evidence we have for alcohol and coffee in relation to cancer.
 “Further research needs to better understand the mechanisms involved in the links between coffee and cancer as well as between alcohol and different cancer subtypes.”
 She said people who were concerned about how their diet might affect their risk of cancer could consult the WCRF’s  cancer prevention recommendations, which advocate limiting alcohol consumption, being a healthy weight and physically active, and enjoying a diet rich in wholegrains, vegetables, fruit and pulses.
 Alcohol increases the risk of cancer because when it is metabolised, it breaks down into chemicals that can bind to DNA, resulting in mutations that could become cancerous.
 Alcohol can also increase the levels of the hormones linked to the development of some types of breast cancer. In general, the more alcoholic drinks a person consumes, the higher their risk of developing one of the associated cancers, the scientists found.
 The authors of the study are calling for more targeted public health policies that enable people to understand the  link between drinking alcohol and cancer to encourage them to limit their consumption.
 Coffee, conversely, is thought to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may protect against diseases triggered by inflammation such as cancer.
 The Imperial study looked at data from 860 reviews of published studies, which explored the association between food and nutrient intake and the risk of either developing or dying from 11 different cancers.
 Although the researchers found weak links between cancer and most types of food and drink, the evidence for increased risk from alcohol consumption and reduced risk for coffee and dairy products was strong for the 11 cancers they looked at.
title: 研究发现，酒精与癌症的相关性比想象的要多
title_en: Alcohol linked to more cancers than thought, study finds

写完《Praise for Alpine Linux》之后，我决定继续写更多的文章来赞美好软件。今天，我想告诉你一些关于 PostgreSQL 的事情。很多人不明白 Postgres 到底有多老：第一个版本 1 是在 1996 年 7 月。它使用了这个标志：经过 25 年的坚持和更好的标志设计，Postgres 成为今天取得巨大成就的最重要的支柱之一在自由软件中，以及 Linux 和 Firefox 之类的软件。 PostgreSQL 已经解决了一个复杂的问题，并以如此有效的程度解决了它，以至于它的所有竞争对手基本上都已经过时了，也许除了 SQLite。首先，Postgres 只是一个非常强大、健壮和可靠的软件，它提供了 SQL 的最佳实现。 2它提供了对其自身行为的大量洞察，并允许有经验的操作员对其进行微调以实现最佳性能。它支持广泛的 SQL 功能和数据类型，我一直能够使用它们高效地存储和检索我的数据。 SQL 通常是 Web 应用程序中的#1 瓶颈，而 Postgres 在为您提供管理该瓶颈所需的工具方面做得非常出色。这些工具也有很好的文档记录。 PostgreSQL 文档非常深入。它让我们其他人感到羞耻，真的。他们不仅有详尽地描述每个特性的全面参考文档，而且还有大量解释 Postgres 的内部设计、架构和操作的散文，以及关于如何完成各种高级任务的详细通俗英语解释，并附有必要的了解这些任务的背景。基本上没有理由阅读关于如何使用 Postgres 做某事的博客文章或 Stack Overflow 的回答——官方文档深入地涵盖了系统的各个方面。该项目由一支纪律严明的工程师团队维护。我对他们处理性能、回归测试和安全性问题的能力充满信心。他们每周发布详尽的开发更新以及详尽的发行说明，让您拥有足够的知识来自信地在部署中运行更新。他们的 gitdiscipline 也是传奇——这是撰写本文时的最新提交： postgres_fdw：修复外部表中生成列的问题。插入外部表时插入列“foo”，因为它试图将值插入到生成的列中。为了解决这个问题，我们假设 postgres_fdwforeign 表中的生成列被定义为代表底层远程表中的生成列：* 在插入或更新时将生成列的 DEFAULT 发送到外部服务器，而不是计算生成的列值在本地服务器上。* 向 postgresImportForeignSchema() 添加一个选项“import_generated”以在从外部服务器导入的外部表的定义中包含列生成的表达式。该选项默认为 true。该假设似乎是合理的，因为这将查询与生成表达式一致的生成列的 postgres_fdw 外部表返回值。同时，修复 postgresImportForeignSchema() 中的另一个问题：它试图包含列当启用 import_default 选项时，生成的表达式作为外部表定义中的列默认表达式。来自 Daniel Cherniy 的错误 #16631。回补到 v12，其中添加了生成的列。讨论：https://postgr.es/m/16631-e929fe9db0ffc7cf%40postgresql.org
归根结底，PostgreSQL 是一个技术复杂的程序，需要经验丰富且熟练的操作员才能有效。学习使用它是一项昂贵的投资，即使它的回报丰厚。尽管 Postgres 偶尔让我感到沮丧或困惑，但总的来说，我对它的感觉是非常积极的。这是一款制作精良的产品，其巨大且仍在不断增长的成功是当之无愧的。当我想到对自由软件生态系统和整个世界产生最重大影响的项目时，PostgreSQL 在该列表中占有一席之地。
note_en:
After writing  Praise for Alpine Linux, I have decided to continue writingmore articles in praise of good software. Today, I’d like to tell you a bitabout  PostgreSQL.
 Many people don’t understand how old Postgres truly is: the first release  1was in July of 1996. It used this logo:
  After 25 years of persistence, and a better logo design, Postgres stands todayas one of the most significant pillars of profound achievement in free software,alongside the likes of Linux and Firefox. PostgreSQL has taken a complex problemand  solved it to such an effective degree that all of its competitors areessentially obsolete, perhaps with the exception of SQLite.
 For a start, Postgres is simply an incredibly powerful, robust, and reliablepiece of software, providing the best implementation of SQL.  2It provides a great deal of insight into its own behavior, and allowsthe experienced operator to fine-tune it to achieve optimal performance. Itsupports a broad set of SQL features and data types, with which I have alwaysbeen able to efficiently store and retrieve my data. SQL is usually the #1bottleneck in web applications, and Postgres does an excellent job of providingyou with the tools necessary to manage that bottleneck.
 Those tools are also exceptionally well-documented. The  PostgreSQLdocumentation is  incredibly in-depth. It puts the rest of us to shame,really. Not only do they have comprehensive reference documentation whichexhaustively describes every feature, but also vast amounts of prose whichexplains the internal design, architecture, and operation of Postgres, plusdetailed plain-English explanations of how various high-level tasks can beaccomplished, complete with the necessary background to  understand thosetasks. There’s essentially no reason to ever read a blog post or Stack Overflowanswer about how to do something with Postgres — the official docs coverevery aspect of the system in great depth.
 The project is maintained by a highly disciplined team of engineers. I havecomplete confidence in their abilities to handle matters of performance,regression testing, and security. They publish meticulously detailed weeklydevelopment updates, as well as thorough release notes that equips you withsufficient knowledge to confidently run updates on your deployment. Their gitdiscipline is also legendary — here’s the  latest commit at the timeof writing:
 postgres_fdw: Fix issues with generated columns in foreign tables.postgres_fdw imported generated columns from the remote tables as plaincolumns, and caused failures like "ERROR: cannot insert a non-DEFAULTvalue into column "foo"" when inserting into the foreign tables, as ittried to insert values into the generated columns. To fix, we do thefollowing under the assumption that generated columns in a postgres_fdwforeign table are defined so that they represent generated columns inthe underlying remote table:* Send DEFAULT for the generated columns to the foreign server on insert or update, not generated column values computed on the local server.* Add to postgresImportForeignSchema() an option "import_generated" to include column generated expressions in the definitions of foreign tables imported from a foreign server. The option is true by default.The assumption seems reasonable, because that would make a query of thepostgres_fdw foreign table return values for the generated columns thatare consistent with the generated expression.While here, fix another issue in postgresImportForeignSchema(): it triedto include column generated expressions as column default expressions inthe foreign table definitions when the import_default option was enabled.Per bug #16631 from Daniel Cherniy. Back-patch to v12 where generatedcolumns were added.Discussion: https://postgr.es/m/16631-e929fe9db0ffc7cf%40postgresql.org
  Ultimately, PostgreSQL is a technically complex program which requires anexperienced and skilled operator to be effective. Learning to use it is a costlyinvestment, even if it pays handsomely. Though Postgres has occasionallyfrustrated or confused me, on the whole my feelings for it are overwhelminglypositive. It’s an incredibly well-made product and its enormous andstill-growing successes are very well-earned. When I think of projects whichhave made the most significant impacts on the free software ecosystem, and onthe world at large, PostgreSQL has a place on that list.
title: 赞美 PostgreSQL

我一直不清楚为什么一本书需要前言。如果原因是书的正文不言自明，那么前言的必要性对于书的正文来说不是很好。如果原因是这本书需要某种形式的认可，那么前言实际上应该由图书销售人员（即明显感兴趣的一方）编写。 （无论如何，没有人会被表面上“无私的政党”所愚弄。）我真的没有能力为 Paolo Soleri 的书写一个传统的前言：首先，因为我不确定我是否理解它——所以我不可能用权威。 （我对此感到敬畏。）第二，因为我不是一个无私的一方；我是他的朋友。索莱里是谁？回答：他是一个身材魁梧、中等身材的男人，大约 50 岁，出生在意大利北部的都灵，在那里获得了建筑学博士学位。二战后，他来到美国，成为弗兰克·劳埃德·赖特的学徒。他是赖特最聪明的两三个学生之一，因此被大师踢了出去。从那时起，他或多或少地在亚利桑那州斯科茨代尔生活和工作，写作、绘画（在无尽的屠夫纸上）和建筑——无论是在斯科茨代尔自己的土地上，还是在美国和欧洲的其他地方。他制作陶瓷和金属铃铛，在微风中摇摆，发出悦耳的声音，以此养活自己和家人。他想说什么？答：我不完全确定，因为这是一本非常难读的书。像许多所谓的有远见的类型一样，索莱里发明了自己的语言，该语言中的一些词在任何英语词典中都找不到。 （奇怪的是，其中一些话有点让人想起五十年前意大利未来主义者的谈话。）我认为他想说的是：在此基础上生长的有机体的结构和性质有一个内在的逻辑行星。任何违反结构和自然的建筑、任何城市设计和任何社会秩序都是对自身和我们的破坏性。任何基于有机原则的建筑、城市设计或社会秩序都是有效的，并将证明其自身的有效性。他想画什么？回答：一个基于这些原则的新世界。而且也很漂亮！更重要的是，这个新世界的第一个定居点似乎很快就会在距离亚利桑那州凤凰城约 70 英里的 4,000 英亩土地上升起，位于海拔 3,700 英尺的可爱高原上。在这里，Soleri 和他的学生——最终总共多达 2,000 人——将很快建立 Arcosanti，一个基于本书中制定的原则的社区。用 Soleri 的话来说，这将是一所城市研究的自我测试学校，一个在实际上就是课程本身的环境中进行教学和生活的地方。这是一个大胆的项目，需要资金支持；但据我所知，它是美国目前唯一一个以真正的力量而不是抵押贷款的远见卓识开始规划的新城。他是实际还是疯了？回答：鉴于近年来这个星球上发生的事情，可以肯定地说，负责人既不实际也不理智。当然，这并不意味着任何对当前事物秩序进行深刻批判的有远见的人都必然更实际或更理智。这只是意味着，任何致力于以目前的方式建造建筑物、城市或社会的人都应该取消自己作为索莱里提议的批评者的资格。在他更清晰的陈述之一中，索莱里说，对公民的关怀是这座城市的精华。但一个人只能关心他所爱的。一座可爱的城市，是一座充满活力的城市的关键。可爱的城市不是偶然的，可爱的人也不是偶然的。我相当怀疑纽约市规划委员会是否可能将 LOVE 作为其总体规划；但如果只是那样做，谁又能说爱不是比委员会目前所遵循的更实际和更明智的政策呢？为什么有人应该阅读索莱里的书？回答：因为这是获得一些非常出色的想法的最佳方式——这些想法将挑战我们其他人日复一日所做的几乎所有事情。 Soleri 说，城市和蚁丘之间的根本区别不仅在于分数，还在于分数。这本书是一个非凡的头脑的作品。我一直在想安东尼奥·圣埃利亚，据我所知，他根本没有建造任何东西，但他描绘的理想城市深刻地塑造了世界上每一个现代城市。
无论如何，最近我还没有看到一本关于建筑和城市设计的书，像这本书一样让我烦恼。如果这是一个不恰当的前言，那就这样吧。这本书的书面内容确实是对我自己和我自己的一种内生的事情。在知识和信息方面，我的资历都非常有限，但必须承认，弧形学的概念标准是构成它们的必要条件。如果我在没有理论背景的情况下展示弧形学，我不会为概念过程提供任何基础，也不会为结果提供任何全面的理由。通过将自己限制在我自己的现实模型的呈现中，我将呈现一个支离破碎的论文，并且无法跟进建设性的建议。或许有很多人比我更有资格阐述一个可信或可用的现实模型。据我所知，没有人意识到这里提出的论文对环境的影响，更不用说在部署任何提案时做出认真的努力了。该论文要求在不玷污或毁坏其自身宇宙方面的情况下对地球进行变形。专业人士、工程师、建筑师和规划师的表现是宇宙现象背后的涂鸦，不会做。无限涂鸦会产生肮脏。事实上，从非常务实的意义上说，无关紧要的大规模入侵是文明齿轮中足以喷出邪恶的沙子。混乱的指数级野蛮是错误秩序的结果，秩序被剥夺了结构。这项工作寻求用更一般的术语对问题进行定义，一个适合人类物种的环境，一种可能在历史上有效的连贯性。为了让读者参与这个过程的连续性并意识到它的宇宙尺度（因此只在这些维度内有意义），已经使用了重申这一担忧的术语。为了自我澄清的目的，一种理论因此与规划思想平行发展。理论本身就是一个参考框架，相当中立。人们对它的使用衡量其有效性。尽管不断坚持理论有时会使头脑迟钝，但这是一种寻求和实现工作连贯性的方式。一种方法是通过将理论和新想法嵌入另一个来测试理论和新想法。接下来是这样一个框架，其巨大的局限性并没有完全抵消作者从其个人构建中获得的优势。为了帮助读者理解这里介绍的材料，我采用了两种设备。首先是使用图表来粗略地说明概念。第二个是对我认为承载或浓缩了想法的书面部分使用斜体。读者可以不间断地浏览斜体部分，然后返回到完整的文本。每一章都是在不同的场合写成的，这是对缺乏连续性的部分解释。对学生进行警告是必要的。图形不能按字面意思理解。象征意义是显而易见的，其特点是帮助生产它的不同的手。在任何情况下，系统的复杂性都会排除在本书应该保留的一般上下文中深思熟虑的细节的可能性。 “只有从物理学、化学、生物学和地质学中提升才能理解人。换句话说，他首先是一个宇宙问题。” Teilhard de Chardin “Reflections of Energy” 科学问题评论（原文）（1952 年 10 月）
“……我们从中获得如此多的骄傲的力量，它实际上是什么，只是人类与物理宇宙的渐进结合的主观意识，伟大的决定论现在在其中发挥作用，不再是强大的局外人，而是通过思想本身，将我们殖民为一个沉默的世界，而我们已成为其中的代理人？” (原文)  Claude Lévi-Strauss Tristes Tropiques (1955) 这项工作有一个关键部分和一个建设性部分。它们在第一部分被同时处理，其中批评与我认为的观点相结合，表明了更大的连贯性。为了帮助集中问题，我大大简化了它。我不认为这会使结果无效，因为这个论点是在桃子不是土豆和桃子不是黄色或桃红色的情况下进行的。其结果不是城市文明的蓝图，而只是通往新选择的指南。在第一部分之后，根据理论命题制定了 30 种方案，然后是 Cosanti 基金会在 Arcosanti 提出的微观实验。在批判性调查中，假设当代人的最大希望已经实现，城市媒体已经清除了贫民窟，清除了疾病和不满。如果没有提到种族隔离、贫民窟清理、剥削者和被剥削者、税收不公、老板主义、儿童被运货卡车杀害、贫民窟游荡、不允许养宠物、利润激励、自我保护等等。帮助，这是因为人们认为人类的技能会及时解决所有问题。股权的基础因此被授予。在城市和郊区的全面协调中，就有了快乐的人。 Megalopoly从上到下，从左到右延伸到各大洲，在无尽而幸福的郊区上空出现胜利和闪亮。这项工作的关键目的是驳斥这种情况的真相；也就是说，这样的希望是双重错误的。它是错误的，因为它不可行；它的内在矛盾和与生活原则的脱节是乌托邦式的。它在致命的意义上是错误的，这是一种比当今肮脏更严重的祸害。当把大城市和郊区作为环境主体时，生活的主体就被否定了。它们的存在和假设的有效性充其量只是外围的，是更实质结构的虚假边缘。然后探究这种结构可以定义的协调环境的构建。本书第一部分所隐含的质疑旨在验证我们给自己设定的理想的有效性。争论是，这种理想是人的价值受克制和部分不自觉地放弃充实生活的结果，它从根本上说是一个邪恶的、反生命的命题。这项工作的建设性目的不是找到一个胆小的替代方案或一个包治百病的答案。所尝试的是澄清人类和社会生活似乎演变的一些非常基本的过程，然后，使用这些概念，努力广泛地定义一些与所达到的基本原理平台相一致的具体有机体，一个平台被视为历史有效。所采取的立场是进化论者。也就是说，我几乎不怀疑一般的生活，特别是人类的生活，可以用向量来象征，而不能用随机模式来象征。矢量性是活生生的现实的特征，而对人的关怀基本上是一种有意或无意识的行动，无论是反对它还是反对它。 （图 9、10）反向量力在无限的熵库中聚集。证明者的力量正在努力通过一个过程来减少和最终消耗这些水库，这个过程在这里被称为真实、过程和目的的美学发生，它们本身就是生命的东西。在这种情况下，乌托邦是对可能存在于人类物种中的任何矢量冲动的否定，以及对不引人入胜的事物的解决，例如，在平静的富裕海洋中放弃。康斯坦丁·多夏迪斯 (Constantine Doxiadis) 展示了一张可怕的世界地图。美洲大陆上覆盖着一种织物，让人联想到强壮躯干所穿的深色破烂法兰绒。洞很大，但只是洞。 （图1）深色的面料是ecumenopoly，普遍的城市。这些洞是地球呼吸和自我更新的区域：绝望的地图。 Doxiadis 预测一个大陆将变成人类的后院。到处都是人蚁，到处都是人蚁。这些漏洞也不会幸免。它们不会是一片片农田或荒野。他们将被子殖民地点缀，每周都会被精神分裂的度假者的浪潮入侵，并季节性地被一群气馁的郊区居民所淹没，他们暂时游牧。
Doxiadis 预言的生物和精神物质的准完整膜将被人造的非生物用具保护。黄金时代的雅典和佛罗伦萨将被印成数千份——一万份代表十亿份，十万份代表一百亿份，依此类推。思维是一种绑定在表面存在的生物的思维。他得到了一个包裹着一个实心球体的表面，他正在尽最大努力用一层、两层或五层的性能来覆盖它。它不会做。一百到五百层的性能将导致整体垄断收缩到其建议规模的百分之一。而不是停滞和过于广泛的伪城市环境层，然后会有密集的活力纤维在大陆和海洋上运行，在地球表面形成肋骨。 （图2）它们实际上是城市河流，其核心是交通和通讯，其顶部是无尽的机场，两侧是实际的城市实体。在地基及其周围，生产工厂将被控制论组织起来。从城市河流中起飞，或进入它们，将是结束或起飞大型模块化城市的较小维度的城市系统，这本书的主题。这里提供的材料是对模块化城市系统的示意性参考，这些城市系统作为附录和城市河流的起源、大部分文明的载体找到了完全的一致性。加速和减速的要求，运动中的物质所特有的奴役，不是强加于光的奴役，最终迫使物质不可避免地需要小型化。小型化是最小化物理世界主要障碍的过程：时空限制。这个定律或规则是如此普遍，以至于指导调查可能最终收敛于物质和能量的普遍物质中出现的明显分歧以及生与死之谜。小型化或死亡一直是早期生活的关键规则。由于这项工作的主题是城市，因此人们可能会在此预期，而不仅仅是比喻性的，小型化或死亡是并且仍然是城市和城市中包含的社会集体动物发展的必要条件世界。世俗化，我们现在的存在标志，将因我们城市中心物理小型化的出现与否而被允许或拒绝。这一事件不仅是成功的关键，而且在这个历史的结合点上，目标的演变也已经确立。可以争辩说，没有什么新事物会随着时间的流逝而发生，如果不是某个特定且恒定的极性正在越来越复杂的系统中缓慢地产生越来越多的表演集群。活的有机体将是来自宇宙四个角落的许多相容事件的集合。构想将是这种内爆过程的触发因素。在心灵的情况下，无限的可能内爆到有限的现实中，它找到发展的食物越远，网络中捕获的数据越多，它的力量就越大，也就是说，它的力量越大。是它的小型化能力，而这又取决于其自身的小型化程度。就好像一步一步的进化会考虑到自身，并对其成就进行完整的概要（小型化），以便在其指尖掌握下一次飞跃的所有可用力量（图 3），这表明普遍性或整体性的容器必须是现有最好工具的小型化。从某种意义上说，有物质的小型化和性能的小型化。一个应对未来的挑战达到另一个程度所允许的成功程度（反之亦然）。
进化暗示了一种脉动行为，它为从物质到思想的每一步设计了一个微型化过程：从无休止的宇宙存在到地质物质的微型化，从地质物质到有机物的微型化，从有机体到有机体的微型化，从有机体到动物的微型化，小型化从动物性到反射性（人）。既然人类正在对人类群体的超有机体进行全面表征，则必须进行另一次小型化。由于进展中固有的递增复杂性，所有这些连续的小型化都是必要的。复杂性越大，表现的空间和时间障碍就越大。因此，在空间和时间上，将更多融入更少的必要性成比例地更大。此外，可以区分两种小型化：一般小型化——从可能到真实；特定的小型化——真实的进化复杂化。 ( 图 4) 如果宇宙是一台确定性的机器，那么现在普遍的小型化就特别属于人，因为他的好奇心使在许多可能性中的选择不仅仅是理论上的决定。微型化为真实，但不进行具体的自我微型化的，成为历史的死肢之一，化为乌有。人类社会面临着这种危险，而由于人类生存的规模相对于地球的大小，人类也在直接危及地球本身的存在。在内心深处，任何有机体都是一个极好的装置，一个复杂、紧凑和一致的奇迹——一个微型化的宇宙。从表面上看，作为一个群体的一员，它只不过是一个笨重机制的一小部分。构成社会的超动物并没有经历小型化的蜕变，而且就其本质而言，它完全没有准备好执行其指定的任务。社会必须成为能够充分发挥作用的真正有机体。这将通过小型化的力量实现。其容器城市的物理小型化是实现这一目标的必要步骤。如果我们不能特别成功地将我们的社会小型化，我们的社会已经从人类物种中的可能存在通过普遍小型化阶段变为基本具体的条件，那么这个社会将不会加入探索进化的存在维度.社会还没有被赋予有机体特征的自我永存的耐力。它必须不断地推理或被迫接受自己的存在
